channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Japan's leader says he's preparing for tough negotiations with Trump on maintaining US presence
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, struggling to stabilize his minority government, said Friday he will seek to maintain regional security and prepare for tough negotiations with U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Afghan women’s group hails court's move to arrest Taliban leaders for persecution of women
item.description: An Afghan women’s group on Friday hailed a decision by the International Criminal Court to arrest Taliban leaders for their persecution of women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tennessee Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Judge to weigh genetic evidence and search warrants in University of Idaho quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Attorneys for a man charged with murder in connection with the killings of four University of Idaho students are asking a judge to throw out most of the evidence in the case because they say it all hinges on an unconstitutional genetic investigation process.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The investigation into why a Nashville high school student fatally shot a classmate before killing himself has centered on his online writings, which authorities describe as concerning warning signs.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Schumer calls on Trump to declassify government files on UFOs
item.description: “Now do UFOs,” Schumer wrote on social platform X in a repost of the White House’s announcement that Trump has ordered government files on John F.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: The officer "had difficulty removing" the gun, and while trying to do so, it fired, striking the driver in the leg, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: An executive order President Donald Trump signed on his first day back in office offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people nationwide.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: High school football player returns to gym after losing arm in ATV accident
item.description: "Being active again has been great for me. It's been great for my mental just getting back to the grind and the normal things I was doing before," Trooper Wright said.
item.dc:creator: Justin Lundy, NewsNation

item.title: Greensboro neighborhood rescues pregnant dog in winter weather
item.description: “She just kept going door to door, up driveways, up on porches, and somebody noticed her and called the shelter,” John Reiland said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Fambro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration blocks federal health agencies from communicating with public
item.description: The Trump administration has put a freeze on many federal health agency communications with the public through at least the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and JONEL ALECCIA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Biden clemency for 'non-violent' inmates includes Connecticut child killer
item.description: Adrian Peeler convicted of conspiracy to murder in case that left a mother and her son dead before they could testify against his brother, another killer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With Trump pivot back to pro-oil and gas policies, one renewable energy finds favor
item.description: As promised, President Donald Trump began reversing the country's energy policies his first day in office with a spate of orders largely favoring oil, gas and coal. But there is one renewable energy that did find favor: geothermal.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes visits Capitol Hill after Trump clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes, who was convicted of orchestrating his far-right extremist group’s Jan. 6, 2021 assault on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta is pushing back on claims that Facebook and Instagram users are being forced into following accounts for President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Judge Chutkan: Trump pardons won't change 'tragic truth' of Jan. 6 Capitol riot
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan, who oversaw President Trump's federal election subversion case, wrote in court filings that his sweeping pardons for those charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021 Capitol attack could not change the “tragic truth” of that day.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon orders 1,500 troops to secure U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will begin deploying as many as 1,500 active duty troops to help secure the southern border in the coming days, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Trump orders FAA to terminate DEI hiring efforts
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday issued an executive order directing the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to "return to non-discriminatory, merit-based hiring" and eliminate diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans renew efforts to limit people in US illegally from census count
item.description: Republican efforts to exclude people in the U.S. illegally from numbers used to divvy up congressional seats among states have begun anew, with four Republican state attorneys general suing to alter the once-a-decade head count even before President Donald Trump’s second term in office began Monday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security Tuesday, firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up and eliminated all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont
item.description: A U.S. Border Patrol agent was shot and killed in northern Vermont on Monday afternoon, the FBI said in a statement later that evening.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends prayer service at National Cathedral
item.description: After being sworn in on Monday, President Donald Trump marked his first full day in office with a prayer service at Washington National Cathedral on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, Fox 5

item.title: Democrat congressman condemns 'what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute' from Musk
item.description: Rep. Jerry Nadler (D-N.Y.) was among those who sharply criticized billionaire Elon Musk on Monday for making "what appears to be a Heil Hitler salute" during an event celebrating the inauguration of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, ABC 4

item.title: Former Planned Parenthood president, women's rights activist Cecile Richards has died at 67
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Cecile Richards, a national leader for abortion access and women’s rights who led Planned Parenthood for 12 tumultuous years, has died. She was 67. Richards died Monday at home in New York “surrounded by family and her ever-loyal dog, Ollie,” her family said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4
